response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong individual contributor with genuine developer tooling credentials — CI/CD infrastructure, test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), mobile device orchestration, and MCP tooling — and a commendable history of knowledge-sharing through workshops and talks. However, the role is explicitly a full-time people-leadership position, and the resume contains zero evidence of people management, team leadership over direct reports, or organizational change experience. Every role listed is an individual contributor title (Software Developer, Staff Software Engineer), and no management scope, headcount ownership, or team-building narrative appears anywhere. The minimum bar of 2 years in people management is simply not met, making this a misaligned candidacy regardless of technical quality.
strengths: Hands-on developer tooling experience including CI/CD setup, test runners, and mobile device orchestration aligns with the team's focus area
Demonstrated pattern of knowledge-sharing via community workshops and conference talks signals potential for team development and mentorship
Broad polyglot and cross-platform background (web, mobile, embedded, systems) provides technical credibility needed to guide and review diverse engineering work
concerns: No people management experience anywhere in the resume — zero evidence of owning headcount, conducting performance reviews, or developing direct reports
No formal technical leadership role; 'Staff' titles at Altom and re:cinq appear to be individual contributor positions without reported teams
No experience leading organizational change or setting strategic roadmaps at team or department level, both of which are explicit requirements

key_factors.factor: No technical leadership or org-change experience
